summary.intent: Address a fourth round of Codex automated review feedback on PR #694 for RatingGroup and TagGroup components in the ars-ui repository, fixing six newly identified behavioral gaps in ARIA semantics and state management.
summary.outcome: All six Codex review threads were fixed, committed (with snapshot updates accepted), and pushed. Review threads were replied to and resolved, and a fresh @codex review was triggered. The fix commit addressed: empty TagGroup focus, removal selection notification, whole-mode radio checked state, selectable grid aria-selected=false, fractional-step slider selection, and root live-region semantics.

repo: The repository uses a Codex automated review workflow triggered by @codex review comments; status is tracked via GitHub PR reactions (👀 = review running, reaction drop = complete).
cargo insta is used for snapshot testing; ARIA output changes require iterative `cargo insta accept` cycles until all snapshots stabilize.
The custom `cargo xci-fast` command runs fmt, check, clippy, and unit suite as the pre-push validation gate.
The snapshot-reviewed label must be re-applied after any commit that modifies .snap files.
TagGroup tracks machine-removed keys separately from prop-synced collections to prevent removed tags from resurrecting on unrelated prop updates.
RatingGroup slider value text must include the rating count for fractional values, not just the raw float.

code.finding: TagGroup focus management after last tag removal needed fixing; root element must receive focus when the tag list becomes empty.
Controlled TagGroup selection must record requested_selected_keys and emit SelectionChange effects for adapter notification.
Selectable tags require aria-selected=false on unselected items, not omission of the attribute.
Whole-mode radiogroup needs correct aria-checked state on radio items; fractional slider mode selection had a gap in handling.
Root live-region semantics (aria-live) were missing from the RatingGroup root element.

workflow: When a review cycle introduces snapshot drift, run `cargo insta accept` multiple times iteratively until the test suite stabilizes — a single accept pass may not catch all pending snapshots.
Codex review polling via GitHub reactions (👀 presence) is an effective but slow synchronization mechanism; 2-minute polling intervals were used, with multiple cycles before the next verdict arrived.
Test variable names must not shadow local helper functions (e.g., naming a binding `service` when a `service()` helper exists in scope causes a compile error).
Commit trailers for co-authorship (Co-authored-by) should be in a separate -m flag from the main commit message when using git commit.

friction: Codex review took multiple polling cycles (10+ minutes of waiting) before completing, requiring repeated sleep+poll loops.
Snapshot tests required three separate `cargo insta accept` + rerun cycles before fully stabilizing after ARIA attribute changes.
A test variable name collision with a local `service()` helper caused a compile failure mid-fix cycle, requiring a patch before tests could run.
The snapshot-reviewed label must be manually re-applied after every commit touching .snap files, which is easy to forget.
open_items: A fifth Codex review pass was triggered at the end of the session but no verdict was received — further review findings may still be pending.
The polling mechanism for Codex review status is manual and error-prone; automating this with a webhook or GitHub Actions step would reduce friction.
Selectable grid aria-selected=false behavior may need broader audit beyond TagGroup if the same pattern is used in other components.
